forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,24 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- claude-code
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: '80'
+title: Short human-friendly task IDs derived from ULID
+---
+Add stable short, human-friendly task IDs for display and reference, derived deterministically from each task's existing ULID. Storage and the canonical identity stay the full ULID — short IDs are a presentation/lookup convenience only.
+
+**Motivation**: Today every reference to a task uses the full 26-char ULID (e.g. `01KT4CNAYW7JG0X8F8W28RFP1R`), which is painful to type or read in commit messages, chat, and CLI args. Inspired by kenn-io/kata, which renders `kata#abc4` — a short ID derived from the issue's ULID.
+
+**Scope**:
+- Derive a short ID deterministically from the ULID (e.g. last N base32 chars), stable for the life of the task — never stored as a separate source of truth, always recomputable.
+- Render the short ID wherever tasks are displayed (CLI output, TUI, task cards). Optionally prefix like `#abc4`.
+- Accept the short ID anywhere a task ID is accepted as input (get/move/complete/etc.), resolving it back to the full ULID. Handle collisions: if a short ID is ambiguous across existing tasks, require disambiguation or fall back to the full ULID.
+
+**Out of scope**: changing stored identity, the JSONL filenames, or the depends_on/attachment reference format (those stay ULID).
+
+**Acceptance**:
+- A task can be fetched/moved/referenced by its short ID and by its full ULID.
+- Short ID is stable and recomputed from the ULID (no new persisted field as source of truth).
+- Collision behavior is defined and tested (real two-task collision, not just a unit of the hash).
+
+Reference: https://github.com/kenn-io/kata — "short IDs derived from each issue's ULID".
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -5,20 +5,31 @@
 position_ordinal: '80'
 title: Short human-friendly task IDs derived from ULID
 ---
-Add stable short, human-friendly task IDs for display and reference, derived deterministically from each task's existing ULID. Storage and the canonical identity stay the full ULID — short IDs are a presentation/lookup convenience only.
+Add stable, short, human-friendly task IDs for display and reference, derived from each task's ULID. The full ULID stays the canonical stored identity; short IDs are a presentation + lookup convenience.

-**Motivation**: Today every reference to a task uses the full 26-char ULID (e.g. `01KT4CNAYW7JG0X8F8W28RFP1R`), which is painful to type or read in commit messages, chat, and CLI args. Inspired by kenn-io/kata, which renders `kata#abc4` — a short ID derived from the issue's ULID.
+**Motivation**: Every reference today uses the full 26-char ULID (e.g. `01KT4CNAYW7JG0X8F8W28RFP1R`) — painful in CLI args, commit messages, chat, and the AI panel. Inspired by kenn-io/kata (`kata#abc4`).

-**Scope**:
-- Derive a short ID deterministically from the ULID (e.g. last N base32 chars), stable for the life of the task — never stored as a separate source of truth, always recomputable.
-- Render the short ID wherever tasks are displayed (CLI output, TUI, task cards). Optionally prefix like `#abc4`.
-- Accept the short ID anywhere a task ID is accepted as input (get/move/complete/etc.), resolving it back to the full ULID. Handle collisions: if a short ID is ambiguous across existing tasks, require disambiguation or fall back to the full ULID.
+## Settled algorithm

-**Out of scope**: changing stored identity, the JSONL filenames, or the depends_on/attachment reference format (those stay ULID).
+- **Short id = last 7 Crockford-base32 chars of the ULID, lowercased.** Recomputed as `ulid[19..26].to_lowercase()` — NEVER stored as a separate field, always derivable.
+  - Rationale: a ULID's first 10 chars are a millisecond timestamp (low entropy, time-clustered, sorts by time) so a prefix is a poor id. The last 16 chars are uniform random (80 bits); the last 7 (~35 bits) match git's default abbreviation length and are effectively collision-free to ~100k tasks. The tail is already uniform — no hashing needed.

-**Acceptance**:
-- A task can be fetched/moved/referenced by its short ID and by its full ULID.
-- Short ID is stable and recomputed from the ULID (no new persisted field as source of truth).
-- Collision behavior is defined and tested (real two-task collision, not just a unit of the hash).
+- **Sigil: `^` (NOT `#`).** Our filter DSL (`crates/swissarmyhammer-filter-expr/src/parser.rs`) reserves `#`=tag, `@`=actor, `$`=project, `^`=reference. Kata's `#` is our tag sigil. `^` already parses a task reference (`^01ABC` → `Expr::Ref`) and already takes a ULID string, so the short id slots into the existing `^ref` path — filter DSL, pills, and references get it for free. Display as `^8rfp1r`.
+
+- **Uniqueness enforced at creation (no resolution-time fallback).** On `add task`, generate the ULID, compute its last-7, and if it collides with any existing task's short id, regenerate the ULID and retry until unique. This makes the short id a stable, board-unique key for the life of the task — no degrade/lengthen, no adaptive matching, no ambiguity handling at lookup time.
+  - The retry loop lives at the **kanban task-creation layer** (has board context), NOT the board-unaware `Ulid::new()` mint in the entity/store layer.
+
+- **Resolution (input)**: accept, case-insensitively, either the full ULID or the 7-char short id, routed through the existing `^` reference mechanism. A short id maps to exactly one task or none. Fixed length only — no adaptive/variable-length suffix matching.
+
+- **Display**: render `^<short>` (lowercase) wherever a task is shown — CLI output, TUI, AI panel, pills.
+
+**Out of scope**: changing stored identity, JSONL filenames, or the on-disk depends_on/attachment reference format (those stay full ULID).
+
+## Acceptance
+
+- A task can be fetched/moved/referenced by its short id (`^8rfp1r`) and by its full ULID; resolution is case-insensitive.
+- Short id is recomputed from the ULID, never persisted as source of truth.
+- `add task` regenerates the ULID on short-id collision; covered by a test that forces a collision (seed/inject a ULID whose last-7 already exists, assert a different unique id is minted) — not just a unit test of the slice.
+- A lightweight `doctor`/validation check asserts no two existing tasks share a short id (safety net for the 2524 pre-existing tasks minted before this invariant).

 Reference: https://github.com/kenn-io/kata — "short IDs derived from each issue's ULID".
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -3,33 +3,25 @@
 - claude-code
 position_column: todo
 position_ordinal: '80'
-title: Short human-friendly task IDs derived from ULID
+project: short-ids
+title: 'Short IDs: core derivation + board resolver'
 ---
-Add stable, short, human-friendly task IDs for display and reference, derived from each task's ULID. The full ULID stays the canonical stored identity; short IDs are a presentation + lookup convenience.
+Foundation card for the Short Task IDs epic. Pure identity layer that everything else depends on. No UI, no create-time changes here.

-**Motivation**: Every reference today uses the full 26-char ULID (e.g. `01KT4CNAYW7JG0X8F8W28RFP1R`) — painful in CLI args, commit messages, chat, and the AI panel. Inspired by kenn-io/kata (`kata#abc4`).
-
-## Settled algorithm
+## Scope
+- `short_id(ulid) -> String` = last 7 Crockford-base32 chars of the ULID, lowercased: `ulid[19..26].to_lowercase()`. Never stored — always derivable.
+  - Why the tail: a ULID's first 10 chars are a ms timestamp (low entropy, time-clustered); the last 16 are uniform random. Last 7 ≈ 35 bits, matches git's default abbreviation, effectively collision-free to ~100k tasks. Tail is already uniform → no hashing.
+- Board-level resolver: `resolve_short_id(&board, &str) -> Option<TaskId>`. Case-insensitive. Accepts the bare 7-char short id OR a leading `^`. Returns exactly one task or none. Fixed length only — no adaptive/variable-length suffix matching.
+- Live in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban` (has board/task context). Add `short_id` as a derived accessor on the task type.

-- **Short id = last 7 Crockford-base32 chars of the ULID, lowercased.** Recomputed as `ulid[19..26].to_lowercase()` — NEVER stored as a separate field, always derivable.
-  - Rationale: a ULID's first 10 chars are a millisecond timestamp (low entropy, time-clustered, sorts by time) so a prefix is a poor id. The last 16 chars are uniform random (80 bits); the last 7 (~35 bits) match git's default abbreviation length and are effectively collision-free to ~100k tasks. The tail is already uniform — no hashing needed.
-
-- **Sigil: `^` (NOT `#`).** Our filter DSL (`crates/swissarmyhammer-filter-expr/src/parser.rs`) reserves `#`=tag, `@`=actor, `$`=project, `^`=reference. Kata's `#` is our tag sigil. `^` already parses a task reference (`^01ABC` → `Expr::Ref`) and already takes a ULID string, so the short id slots into the existing `^ref` path — filter DSL, pills, and references get it for free. Display as `^8rfp1r`.
-
-- **Uniqueness enforced at creation (no resolution-time fallback).** On `add task`, generate the ULID, compute its last-7, and if it collides with any existing task's short id, regenerate the ULID and retry until unique. This makes the short id a stable, board-unique key for the life of the task — no degrade/lengthen, no adaptive matching, no ambiguity handling at lookup time.
-  - The retry loop lives at the **kanban task-creation layer** (has board context), NOT the board-unaware `Ulid::new()` mint in the entity/store layer.
-
-- **Resolution (input)**: accept, case-insensitively, either the full ULID or the 7-char short id, routed through the existing `^` reference mechanism. A short id maps to exactly one task or none. Fixed length only — no adaptive/variable-length suffix matching.
-
-- **Display**: render `^<short>` (lowercase) wherever a task is shown — CLI output, TUI, AI panel, pills.
-
-**Out of scope**: changing stored identity, JSONL filenames, or the on-disk depends_on/attachment reference format (those stay full ULID).
+## Out of scope (separate cards)
+- Create-time uniqueness / regenerate-on-collision → short-ids-create-uniqueness
+- Tool/CLI input + output → short-ids-tool-api
+- Filter `^ref` eval → short-ids-filter-eval
+- CM6 mention identity / pills / autocomplete → short-ids-mention-identity + the two CM6 cards

 ## Acceptance
+- `short_id` derivation + round-trip unit tests.
+- Resolver tests: exact short id, `^`-prefixed, case-insensitive, not-found, and (given current board) a real lookup against a constructed task.

-- A task can be fetched/moved/referenced by its short id (`^8rfp1r`) and by its full ULID; resolution is case-insensitive.
-- Short id is recomputed from the ULID, never persisted as source of truth.
-- `add task` regenerates the ULID on short-id collision; covered by a test that forces a collision (seed/inject a ULID whose last-7 already exists, assert a different unique id is minted) — not just a unit test of the slice.
-- A lightweight `doctor`/validation check asserts no two existing tasks share a short id (safety net for the 2524 pre-existing tasks minted before this invariant).
-
 Reference: https://github.com/kenn-io/kata — "short IDs derived from each issue's ULID".
\ No newline at end of file

forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -11,17 +11,23 @@
 ## Scope
 - `short_id(ulid) -> String` = last 7 Crockford-base32 chars of the ULID, lowercased: `ulid[19..26].to_lowercase()`. Never stored — always derivable.
   - Why the tail: a ULID's first 10 chars are a ms timestamp (low entropy, time-clustered); the last 16 are uniform random. Last 7 ≈ 35 bits, matches git's default abbreviation, effectively collision-free to ~100k tasks. Tail is already uniform → no hashing.
-- Board-level resolver: `resolve_short_id(&board, &str) -> Option<TaskId>`. Case-insensitive. Accepts the bare 7-char short id OR a leading `^`. Returns exactly one task or none. Fixed length only — no adaptive/variable-length suffix matching.
+- Board-level resolver: `resolve_short_id(&board, &str) -> Option<TaskId>`. Case-insensitive. Accepts the bare 7-char short id, a leading `^`, OR a full 26-char ULID (full → last-7 → lookup). Returns exactly one task or none. Fixed length only — no adaptive/variable-length suffix matching.
 - Live in `crates/swissarmyhammer-kanban` (has board/task context). Add `short_id` as a derived accessor on the task type.

+## Storage policy (canonical)
+- The full 26-char ULID is the ONE stored identity. Every structured reference field that holds a task id — `depends_on`, attachment refs, etc. — STORES the full ULID, never the short id. (Confirmed: depends_on stores full ULIDs.)
+- The short id is display + input only. When a structured ref field is edited via `^` autocomplete (short id entered), normalize the short id back to the full ULID on commit before storing.
+- Exception — free-text bodies (task description markdown): the literal token the user inserted is what's stored, which from autocomplete is `^<short>`. This is safe because short ids are unique-at-create and stable for the task's life; the pill layer re-resolves them on render. No normalization of body text.
+- JSONL on disk and filenames are unchanged.
+
 ## Out of scope (separate cards)
 - Create-time uniqueness / regenerate-on-collision → short-ids-create-uniqueness
-- Tool/CLI input + output → short-ids-tool-api
+- Tool/CLI input + output (incl. ref-field input normalization) → short-ids-tool-api
 - Filter `^ref` eval → short-ids-filter-eval
 - CM6 mention identity / pills / autocomplete → short-ids-mention-identity + the two CM6 cards

 ## Acceptance
 - `short_id` derivation + round-trip unit tests.
-- Resolver tests: exact short id, `^`-prefixed, case-insensitive, not-found, and (given current board) a real lookup against a constructed task.
+- Resolver tests: exact short id, `^`-prefixed, full ULID, case-insensitive, not-found, and a real lookup against a constructed task.

 Reference: https://github.com/kenn-io/kata — "short IDs derived from each issue's ULID".
\ No newline at end of file
